titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert K. Greenleaf Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity School Library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday June 15, 2026 at 18:00

did.abstract: The collection documents Greenleaf's professional life, including as founder of the Servant Leadership movement and of the Greenleaf Center for Servant Leadership, which was first known as the Center for Applied Ethics.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Greenleaf was born in 1904 in Terre Haute, Indiana. He attended Rose Polytechnic (an engineering school) in Terre Haute for several years, then transferred to Carleton College in Minnesota. He graduated as a math major in 1926 and took a job with AT and T (telephone company). During his 38-year career with the company he served as Director of Management Development, originator of the world's first corporate assessment center, promotion of the women and Black workers to non-menial positions, a program to expose up-and-coming leaders to the humanities, even bringing in famous theologians and psychologists to speak about the wider implications of corporate decisions.

Greenleaf retired in 1964 and began his second career as a writer, consultant, and teacher. His seminal essay "The Servant as Leader" was published in 1970. In 1976, Paulist Press published a book of his essays titled "Servant Leadership." He died in 1990.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School Collection, 2017; Gift of Jeffrey D. Jones, 2023

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes autobiographical material, correspondence, subject files, writings, and memorabilia. Correspondence from Jeffrey D. Jones dating from the 1980s was added to the collection in 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I. Journal and Autobiographical Material

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert K. Greenleaf Papers (RG 287), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

did.unittitle: Notebook and loose pages of journal and dream notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from work with Jungian analysts Ira Progoff and Martha Jaeger.

did.unittitle: Two notebooks of dream notes - DB#1, DB#2
Audiocassette tape related to sessions of Greenleaf with Ira Progoff
Notes related to "hole in the hedge philosophy"
Handwritten narrative of life and work through 1920s - 2 drafts
Handwritten narrative: "My work with AT&T" - 2 drafts
Typescript narratives: "My work in education", "My work with churches"
Handwritten and typescript narratives: "My work with foundations"
Handwritten and typescript narratives: "My work in India"
Typescript narrative: "Age 40-60: What I did with those years"
Handwritten narrative of life and work after age 60
Handwritten narrative: "The institution as servant, reflections on experience"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See more correspondence in the Subject Files.

did.unittitle: Correspondence concerning Greenleaf and the Greenleaf Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Winter 1990-91 issue of The Servant Leader, newsletter of the Robert K. Greenleaf Center, with tribute to Greenleaf.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a more detailed listing of contents of subject files see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: The Servant as A Person (Jefferson House)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of titles see original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Abortion"; "The Center City Church as Servant to its Community"; "Center for Advanced Study in Administration"; "Change"; "Christian-Jewish Relations"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Choice is Always Ours"; "Coercion, Manipulation, and Persuasion"; "Community as Servant and Nurturer of the Human Spirit", "Conscience and Achievement"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Direction in the University"; "Ethics in Business"; "The Ethic of Strength"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Foundations and Servants"; "Growing Greatness in Managers"; re. Human Resources; "The Identification of Management Potential"; "India - Persuasive as Power"; "The Inner City Church as Servant to the Community"' introduction to unidentified book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It Depends on the Person"; "It's Your Life"; "Leading vs Structuring"; "A Lifeline of Ideas";"Lost Knowledge"; re. managers; ""Meditation on Power"; "Note on a Chairman's Institute"; "Note to CEO of a large business..."; "Notes on Community in the Late Twentieth Century".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Notes on Institute of Chairing"; re. organizations; "Persuasion as Power"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Power"; "Power in the Executive Office"; "The Prospect for a Servant-Led Society"' "Report on a Journey"; "Report on The Management Work Conference of National Training Laboratories"; "A Sense of History"; "A Set of Organization Ideas to guide Committee on Governance"; re. Society of Friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Some Thoughts About the Top Man"; "Staff to Trustees"; :A Suggested Goal for the Richardson Foundation"; "Suggested Introductory Notes on Ethical Implications of Business Action"; "Talking with People"; "Theology as Concern for the Conditions..."; "3200 A.D - a Phantasy"; "Training for the Lay Ministry"; "Training Management People"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Use of the Undergraduate Years..."; "Visits to Schools of Adminstration"; "What makes an institution distinguished"; "You need to be human"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Industry's Means for Personality Adjustment"; "The Development of Influence Potential. Notes for a talk"; "To Joe" (poem); "Proceedings of panel discussion: Greenleaf presentation: Listening - a Basic Executive Skill"; "Management Development Objectives"; "Behavioral Research: Factor in Tomorrow's Better Management"; "Rough transcript of Talk before the professional staff of a management consulting firm"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Education and Maturity. A Talk before the faculty and students of Barnard College"; "A View of Managerial Ethics"; "Uniqueness, Paradox and Choice: Notes on strategy for potentially successful people"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Revised Proposal for Executive Development for Church Leadership"; "A New Religious Mission"; "Memorandum: The Shortage of Top Administrators"; "An Opportunity for a Powerful New Religious Influence"; "What Makes a Distinguished Management?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Responsibility in a Democratic Society"; "The Search and the Seeker"; 'Education for Leadership"; "Religious Seekers Anonymous"; "Memorandum: A Possible Next Step for Negro Colleges"; "An Issue of Management Talents: The Operator Versus the Conceptualizer"; "Speech to the Fortune 500"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Institutional Design: A Proposal for Recovery of Trust"; "A talk to the Person/Team Section of the National Council of Catholic Laity"; "Notes on the Use of the Olsson Endowment, University of Virginia"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [Function of the Office of General Counsel]; "Proposal for a Chairman's Institute"; "An Observer's View of the Chief Executive Officer's Role"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Note on the Need for a Theology of Institutions and Supplement";"The Seminary as an Institution"; "Spirituality As Leadership"; "Critical Thought in the Seminary and the Trustee Chairperson's Role"; "A Fable"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Reflections on Spirit"; "Reflections From Experience on the subjects of Power, the Uses of History, Nurturing the Human Spirit"; "Pope John XXIII"; "Further Reflections on the Issue of Power/Imagination"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Reflections on the Idea of the Servant As Nurturer of the Human Spirit": "The Idea of the Servant as Nurturer of the Human Spirit"; "Some Reflections on the Question: What Makes a City Great?"; "The Servant Leadership Idea: Where I Got It and Where I Want To Go With It"; "Pope John XXIII: Spirit Nurturer of the World"; "Reflections on Ministry to the Strong"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: untitled; "Church as Servant"; "Manager, Administrator, Statesman"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "An Opportunity for a Foundation Concern in the Field of Leadership"; " Reflections on Experience"; " A Sense of History"; " What are you called to do? A sense of Vocation"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of publications by Greenleaf see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Abraham Joshua Heschel: Build a Life Like a Work of Art"; "Business Ethics - Everybody's Problem"; "Choosing Greatness"; "Choosing to be Aware"; "Choosing to Grow"; "Community as Servant and Nurturer of the Human Spirit"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Doors of Perception"; "The Foundation Trustee"; "Is It More Blessed to Give Than to Receive"; "A Mission for Us"; "Now is the Time to Build Anew"; "On Being a Seeker in the Late Twentieth Century"; "Overcome Evil with Good"' "Prudence and Creativity: A Trustee Responsibility"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Servant as Leader"; "Spirituality as Leadership"; "The Trustee and the Risks of Persuasive Leadership"' "Trustees Consider a Hypothetical Case"; "Two More Choices"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Advices to Servants"; "The Choice is Always Ours"; "Education and Maturity"; "A Forward Look at Management Development"; "Friends Schools and the Issue of Power and Authority"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Have You a Dream Deferred?"; "The Institution as Servant"; "The Leadership Crisis"; "Life Style of Greatness"; "Life's Choices and Markers"; "A Mission for Quaker Men"; "My Debt to E. B. White"; "Planning for Peak Production"; "Responsibility in a Bureaucratic Society"; "Robert Frost's Directive and the Spiritual Journey"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Seminary as Servant"; "The Servant as Leader"; "The Servant as Religious Leader"; "Servant Retrospect and Prospect"; "Trustees as Servants"

did.unittitle: "Creative Realistic Leaders" in Managers for the Year 2000
"Trustee Traditions and Expectations" in The Good Steward: A Guide to Theological School Trusteeship
"Trustees as Servants" in The Role of the Voluntary Trustee
Materials developed for American Telephone and Telegraph Company and Illinois Bell Telephone Company
Reports of various studies and projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Report of the Committee on Structure to members of Council of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants"; "Cornell University Survey"; "The Study of the Humanities as an Approach to Executive Development";

did.unittitle: A Conversation on Servant Leadership with Philip A. Amerson (VHS tape)
The Servant as Leader Idea (VHS tape)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles in Consumers' Cooperation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "An Essay on Lemoyne" by John Dixon; "Barriers and Gateways to Communication" by Rogers and Roethlisberger; " "Retrospect and Prospect" by Henry Pratt Fairchild; "The Mayer Plan for Self-Endowed Communities"; ""The Unicorn in the Garden" by James Thurber; ""The Discomfort of Growth" in Christian Laymen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Future of Teaching" by William Arrowsmith; "Strength in a Religious Society" in The Laymen's Movement Review ; "The Strategy of a Minority" by Elton Trueblood; "Beyond Institutional Mediocrity" by Walt Scarvie; "Master Teacher and Disciples: An Old and Good Model" by Philip Amerson; "Dr. Rupert Sheldrake" by Robert Anton Wilson; "The Ultimate Triumph of the Human Spirit…" by Stephen Cary; "Work Community and the Corporation" by David H. Smith; "Professional Ethics in Higher Education: Methods, Theories and Practices"

did.unittitle: International certificates of vaccination and personal health history: Robert K. and Esther H. Greenleaf
Passports: Robert K. and Esther H. Greenleaf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter of Greenleaf to his mother, report cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Carleton College handbook and directories, letters regarding summer employment and the Men's Glee Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents re marriage license and certificate, poem to commemorate retirement from Dartmouth program

did.unittitle: Material re honorary degree awarded by Carleton College
Obituaries and memorials of Robert K. Greenleaf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including by Ed Ouelette, Alfonso Narvaez, and memorial service program at Bangor Theological Seminary

did.unittitle: Notes for uncompleted indexing of correspondence
Microfilm prepared for preservation purposes by Andover Newton Theological School Library (9 positive and 9 negative reels plus one extra positive copy of reel 7)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labels on the microfilm correlate with the original finding aid for the Greenleaf Papers, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.
